key_insight: Orthogonalized enriched finite elements combine atom-centered enrichment with standard finite elements to deliver a more efficient, scalable basis for pseudopotential Kohn-Sham DFT.
engineering_relevance: The approach cuts degrees of freedom, runtime, and memory substantially, making large-scale electronic-structure simulations more practical on shared HPC resources.
method: Augment the classical finite element basis with compact atom-centered enrichment functions.
Orthogonalize enrichment functions against the underlying CFE basis to improve conditioning and overlap-matrix inversion.
Use residual-based Chebyshev subspace iteration to solve the Kohn-Sham eigenproblem robustly.
evidence_signal: Benchmarks up to 39,083 electrons show a 5-7× reduction in degrees of freedom, 5-9× lower computational cost, and 4-5× less memory versus classical finite elements, with good parallel scalability.
watch_item: Performance depends on reliable approximate overlap-matrix inversion and careful enrichment/orthogonalization setup to preserve numerical stability.
paper_title: Large-scale pseudopotential density functional theory calculations using orthogonalized enriched finite element basis
